description: Letter from Barhun b. Musa, probably from Alexandria, to Nahray b. Nissim, Fustat. Probably 1062. Barhun writes the letter in the name of his brother, Yosef. He does not write the place where the letter is written but it seems like he is in a port city, probably Alexandria. Before that, he was in Safakus and Mahdiyya. Mentions large shipments of dirhams. Several of Nahray’s business partners are mentioned as well. Deals with selling and shipping goods to Mahdiyya, Tripoli, and Sicily and some details about prices. Mentions the Byzantines that want pepper. Also mentions some family matters about Nahray’s aunt (from his mother’ side) who is in a bad financial situation, and his cousin Yisra'el b. Natan who is in mental distress (or is simply engaged in a prolonged dispute with Barhūn; this section begins on verso, line 9). It seems that Barhun wrote the letter soon before his death in 1062, because he mentions the passing of Elhanan b. Isma’il al-Tāhartī, who died in 1062. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #348) VMR
tags: Yisrael b. Natan, Byzantine merchants, Byzantines, trade
description_he: מכתב מברהון בן מוסא, כנראה מאלכסנדריה, לנהראי בן ניסים, פוסטאט, כנראה 1062, על משלוחי דרהמים וסחורות ועל ענייני משפחה
